heroHeadline: Falam Falls Back to Junta After Year-Long Resistance Hold; SCEF Unified Resistance Council Launched; SAC Drones Kill 8 Civilians Including 3 Children
heroSubtitle: On April 27, 2026 — Day 1,911 of Myanmar's post-coup civil war — the resistance launched its most unified political-military structure since the coup: the Steering Council for the Emergence of a Federal Democratic Union (SCEF), formally bringing together the NUG, CRPH, KIO, KNU, KNPP, and CNF. The SCEF launch came one day after China FM Wang Yi concluded his Naypyidaw visit by pledging Beijing would 'firmly support Myanmar in safeguarding its national sovereignty' — providing China's explicit endorsement of the junta's 60-township martial law expansion and continued aerial campaign. Falam, Chin State's capital, was recaptured by SAC forces on April 25–26 after the resistance held it for nearly one year — costing the junta an estimated 500+ casualties; resistance forces withdrew with 300+ captured weapons. SAC drone strikes on April 25–26 killed eight civilians across two regions: five in Nanmaikone Township (Karenni State) and three children including a novice monk aged 16 in Yesagyo Township (Magway). In Karen State, the KNLA launched an assault on Waw Lay military base near Myawaddy, forcing ~20 junta personnel to flee while junta indiscriminate shelling wounded 12+ civilians near the Thai border. On April 24, SAC jets, Mi-35 helicopters, and drones killed 12+ civilians in Bon Taung village, Thandaunggyi Township (Karen State). The Irrawaddy continues to document that the SAC has killed 100+ civilians since its parliament convened on March 16, with the post-'civilian rule' aerial campaign now in its seventh week.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa CDM News, Japan Times, Khaosod English, The Nation Thailand, Foreign Policy, Xinhua, CGTN, SCMP, Free Malaysia Today, BERNAMA, Anadolu Agency, IMNA/Mon News, FDD, China MFA, Shan Herald Agency for News, and official NUG, KNU, KIA, AA, and PDF statements.
